subject: China Challenges AI Dominance as Netflix Buys AI Filmmaking
preview: China's AI giants take on US leaders, while Netflix shells out $587M for Ben Affleck's AI startup.
intro: It's another day, another dollar, and another AI bombshell.
tweet: China's top AI companies release open-source models rivaling OpenAI and Anthropic at lower costs, challenging America's AI lead. Get the 5-minute AI brief, subscribe for free!
linkedin: China's leading AI companies are making waves with open-source models that can rival the best from OpenAI and Anthropic. This shift could potentially narrow America's AI lead. Today, we saw Netflix acquire an AI filmmaking startup and a lawsuit against OpenAI. Don't miss out on the latest AI news, subscribe to our free daily newsletter.

items.headline: China Challenges AI Dominance
items.blurb: China's leading AI companies are releasing open-source models that can rival the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a lower cost. This rapid-fire release suggests America's AI lead is narrowing.

items.headline: Netflix Buys AI Filmmaking Startup
items.blurb: Netflix acquired InterPositive, a startup co-founded by Ben Affleck, for $587 million in cash. The deal highlights the growing importance of AI in filmmaking.

items.headline: Apple Lawsuit Threatens OpenAI
items.blurb: Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI may derail the company's plans to enter the hardware market and go public. The implications for OpenAI's future are uncertain.

items.headline: Beyond Grep: AI Coding Harness
items.blurb: Augment Code's Vinay Perneti discusses the potential of a context-rich AI coding harness, which could revolutionize the way coders work.

items.headline: StrictlyVC Returns to NYC
items.blurb: StrictlyVC is coming back to New York City in September to celebrate the city's thriving startup community. The event promises unparalleled access to the startup and VC world.
